name: Montánchez - Arroyomolinos – circular via the Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27)
name_original: Montánchez - Arroyomolinos – circular por la Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27)
region_intro: Jogging around Montánchez offers diverse terrain within Extremadura's Cáceres Province. The region is characterized by granite hills ranging from 300 to 994 meters, providing varied gradients for running. An extensive network of footpaths, some granite-paved, winds through low-lying pastures, olive and fig trees, and challenging slopes with deciduous oaks and chestnut woods. These trails provide options for different fitness levels, from moderate paths to more demanding routes. Best jogging routes around Montánchez The most popular jogging route is Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) — circular desde Arroyomolinos, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the historic mills and scenic chestnut groves. Another top favourite among local runners is Montánchez - Arroyomolinos – circular via the Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27), a difficult 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This route features similar landscapes to its counterpart, including varied gradients and natural features. Local runners also love the Dolmen de Carmonita Loop, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through scenic dehesas, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Montánchez is defined by granite hills, extensive footpath networks, and chestnut groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Montánchez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes around Montánchez?
A: The Montánchez region, part of Extremadura's Cáceres Province, features diverse terrain characterized by granite hills ranging from 300 to 994 meters in elevation. You'll find an extensive network of footpaths, some granite-paved, winding through low-lying pastures with olive and fig trees, as well as more challenging slopes covered with deciduous oaks and scenic chestnut woods. This variety offers different gradients suitable for various running preferences.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier jog?
A: While many routes in Montánchez are rated moderate to difficult, there are options for those seeking a less strenuous experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346065892/dolmen-de-carmonita-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Carmonita Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) trail that leads through scenic dehesas, offering a more accessible run. The region's extensive footpath network also allows for shorter, less challenging sections to be explored.

Q: Which running trails offer the best panoramic views in Montánchez?
A: Several trails in the Montánchez area are known for their breathtaking panoramic views. The 'Montánchez Antennas Trail' is specifically mentioned for its scenic vistas. Additionally, routes like the 'Hike to Mirador 'el cancho que se menea'' and the 'Turtle Rock – View of Castello loop' provide elevated perspectives, rewarding runners with expansive scenery of the surrounding Extremaduran landscape.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Montánchez?
A: Yes, Montánchez offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280695310/ruta-de-los-molinos-pr-cc-27-circular-desde-arroyomolinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) — circular desde Arroyomolinos&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.9-mile (12.7 km) trail exploring historic mills and chestnut groves. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991216537/montanchez-arroyomolinos-circular-via-the-ruta-de-los-molinos-pr-cc-27" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez - Arroyomolinos – circular via the Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers similar landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346065892/dolmen-de-carmonita-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Carmonita Loop&lt;/a&gt; is also a moderate circular route.

Q: Are there any natural features or landmarks to see along the running routes?
A: Absolutely! The running routes around Montánchez are rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the picturesque chestnut groves, especially vibrant in autumn, and the scenic Garganta de los Molinos gorge. Historical points of interest include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/745456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers commanding views, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346065892/dolmen-de-carmonita-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Carmonita&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Cross on the Camino&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago de Bencáliz Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Montánchez?
A: While jogging is enjoyable year-round, autumn is particularly recommended for running in Montánchez. During this season, the chestnut groves, such as those found along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280695310/ruta-de-los-molinos-pr-cc-27-circular-desde-arroyomolinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Molinos&lt;/a&gt;, offer stunning scenic views with their changing foliage, creating a pleasant and colorful environment for your run.

Q: Are there any longer running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Montánchez offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyards of Montánchez – The Rock That Moves loop from Montánchez&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 9.9-mile (15.9 km) route with significant elevation gain, taking approximately 2 hours and 11 minutes to complete. The extensive network of interconnecting footpaths also allows for combining routes to create all-day excursions.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the running routes available?
A: The running routes around Montánchez cater to various fitness levels, though the majority are classified as moderate to difficult. Out of 17 available routes, 7 are rated moderate and 10 are considered difficult. This means you'll find plenty of options for challenging yourself with varied gradients and terrain, but also some moderate paths for a good workout.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Montánchez?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail are not detailed, the extensive network of footpaths and natural landscapes in the Sierra de Montánchez generally make it a welcoming area for outdoor activities with pets. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to respect local signage regarding pet access.

Q: Are there any facilities or places to rest and refuel near the running routes?
A: The region's villages, including Montánchez itself, provide opportunities to rest and enjoy local produce after your run. While specific cafes or pubs directly on every route are not guaranteed, you can typically find amenities within the villages located throughout the Sierra de Montánchez, making them convenient points for starting or ending your run.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Montánchez area?
A: There are over 15 dedicated running routes available around Montánchez, offering a wide variety of distances, terrains, and difficulty levels for joggers and runners to explore the beautiful Extremaduran landscape.
tips: This picturesque approved route (PR-CC 27) runs through the interior of the Sierra de Montánchez and Tamuja region through a very picturesque landscape of chestnut trees. It has no technical difficulty and can be started from Montánchez or Arroyomolinos.
